Descriptions.ShortDescription: Captures per-exposure telemetry from your NINA imaging sessions and posts it to the Subframes.io API for analysis and tracking.
Descriptions.LongDescription:
# Subframes.io NINA Plugin

A NINA plugin that automatically captures imaging telemetry and sends it to your [Subframes](https://subframes.io/) account after every saved frame.

## What it does

- **Session Start**: Creates a new imaging session via the Subframes API when your sequence begins
- **Per-Frame Data**: After every saved image, posts frame metadata including filter, exposure time, gain, offset, HFR, star count, guiding RMS, and camera temperature
- **Session End**: Closes the session when your sequence ends
- **Station Heartbeat**: Periodically reports your equipment configuration (mount, camera, focuser, filters, location) to Subframes
- **Non-Blocking**: If the API is unreachable, the plugin logs a warning and continues your sequence normally — your imaging is never interrupted

## Requirements

- NINA 3.1 or later
- A [Subframes account](https://app.subframes.io/) with a valid API key

## Setup

1. Open the Subframes panel in NINA (View > Panels > Subframes)
2. Enter your Subframes API Base URL
3. Enter your API Key (starts with `astk_live_`)
4. Enable **Send data to Subframes API** and click **Save Settings**
5. In the Advanced Sequencer, add **Start Subframes Session** to your sequence start container

## Source

Full source code available at [github.com/subframes-astro/nina-plugin](https://github.com/subframes-astro/nina-plugin)
